summary:
The agent initially considered the thumb as potentially excluded due to common misconceptions, citing a possible link between thumb anatomy and variable ridge patterns; however, it later concluded that no supported evidence existed within retrieved documents confirming any specific finger omission during standard eight-finger fingerprinting. Ultimately, because retrieval yielded no documentation related to fingertip selection rules, the correct inference could not be made definitively—even when acknowledging general forensic norms beyond the dataset.  

This summary focuses only on clinically relevant aspects tied to actual fingerprinting methods and avoids speculative interpretations absent factual basis.
